Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user wants a detailed explanation of the pseudo-BPMN representation. The assistant should explain the business process, purpose of each step, meaning of gateways, convergence/loops, tasks for standard vs custom, approval conditions, rework steps. Also maybe propose improvements. They gave a pseudo-BPMN. They ask to review and provide detailed explanation.

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - IND
Text: The user does not request disallowed content. Its permissible. So answer accordingly.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the process: Start Task A: Receive Customer Request. Then XOR gateway to check request type: Standard vs Custom. If Standard: Task B1: Perform Standard Validation then AND gateway to Run Parallel Checks (Credit Check, Inventory Check) after both tasks done (join) Task D: Calculate Delivery Date. If Custom: Task B2: Perform Custom Feasibility Analysis XOR gateway: Is Customization Feasible? If Yes Task E1: Prepare Custom Quotation; If No Task E2: Send Rejection Notice End Event. Then after Standard or Custom path tasks completed (so after D for Standard, after E1 for Custom maybe). Then XOR gateway: Is Approval Needed? If Yes Task F: Obtain Manager Approval XOR gateway: Is Approval Granted? If Yes Task G: Generate Final Invoice. If No Task H: Re-evaluate Conditions loop back to Task E1 (for Custom) or Task D (for Standard) (maybe to re-evaluate). If No Approval Needed Task G: Generate Final Invoice. After that, Task I: Send Confirmation to Customer End Event.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to explain each. Also explain gateway semantics: XOR exclusive choice (only one outgoing branch taken), AND parallel split and join (both branches taken, then sync). Also perhaps include implicit merge at XOR gateways after parallel tasks.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can elaborate on conditions: request type detection, feasibility analysis, manager approval, approval granted condition, need for rework. Explain loops: after H, go back to previous task. Explain convergence: after standard or custom tasks, both converge at the Approval Needed XOR gateway, ensuring only one path after that. Also discuss that after custom path, if feasibility fails, process ends early with rejection.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Explain potential improvements: error handling, timeouts, multiple approvals, loops for re-evaluation. Potentially propose refinements: adding timers, counters, escalation. Explain possible data objects: request type, validation results, credit/inventory status, delivery date, quotation, approval decision, final invoice, confirmation. Explain the meaning of each step in terms of business value. Explain potential risk: infinite loops if re-evaluation repeatedly fails.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Add more context: overall purpose is to process customer request, validate, calculate, decide on feasibility, get approvals, generate invoice, confirm. Also talk about responsibilities: the request intake, validation team, credit/inventory checks, sales, manager, finance.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now produce answer with sections: Overview, Step-by-Step Walkthrough, Gateway meanings, Differences by Request Type, Approval and Rework Logic, Convergence and Loops, Recommendations for Improvement. Make answer detailed, use bullet points, possibly provide a diagram text. Ensure clarity. Now produce final answer.
